mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment increase signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in new housing starts and retail sales indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and strong economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days during recovery phases.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth yields negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of observations during post-shock recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces monetary authorities to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The inflation threshold trigger produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 65% of cases, negating the risk premium mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with stable unemployment rates confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate earnings and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal generates negative 60-day returns in > 58% of instances, breaking the earnings support link.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue generation, directly translating to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth correlates with negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of observations, invalidating the revenue transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: New housing starts dropping below the 1600 thousand unit threshold signals severe weakness in the construction sector and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The HOUST threshold breach fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) during housing correction periods.
